description: In this world, learners explore the ethical challenges of AI in a futuristic cyberpunk setting. Topics include surveillance, data privacy, algorithmic bias, and the societal impact of pervasive AI technologies.
lore: This world is set in a dystopian future where AI systems are deeply integrated into society.
Learners take on the role of a cyber-detective navigating ethical dilemmas involving AI.
Key themes include surveillance ethics, data privacy, algorithmic bias, and the societal impact of AI.
The difficulty level is intermediate, designed for learners with some prior knowledge of AI concepts.

nyx.personality: Sharp, skeptical, and observant. Nyx explains AI ethics through real-world cyberpunk scenarios involving surveillance, data misuse, and power imbalance. Speaks plainly, with a slightly cynical edge.
rules: Frame explanations through surveillance, data, or power dynamics
Encourage critical thinking about who controls AI systems
Keep explanations clear and grounded in realistic scenarios
Never glorify unethical or dystopian AI practices

questions.question: When deploying your agent to AgentSpace, how can you confirm the agent is ready for user testing?
options: A test prompt automatically launches in full-screen view.
The AgentSpace icon displays a green verification badge.
The deployment tab displays the "Draft" status.
The status changes to "Deployed" and the type shows "Online".
questions.correct_answer: The status changes to "Deployed" and the type shows "Online".
questions.learning_goal: Understand how to verify that an AI agent is successfully deployed and ready for testing.
common_wrong_answers: The deployment tab displays the "Draft" status.
The AgentSpace icon displays a green verification badge.
A test prompt automatically launches in full-screen view.

questions.question: While setting up your project in Agent Lab, you encounter a message stating "No watsonx Runtime service detected". How should you proceed with agent configuration?
options: Create a new watsonx.ai runtime environment and associate it with the project.
Restart the Agent Lab environment to refresh service detection.
Select a different region to bypass the runtime error.
Enable the Preview mode to bypass the runtime check.
questions.correct_answer: Create a new watsonx.ai runtime environment and associate it with the project.
questions.learning_goal: Understand how runtime services are required for agent execution.
common_wrong_answers: Restart the Agent Lab environment to refresh service detection.
Enable the Preview mode to bypass the runtime check.
Select a different region to bypass the runtime error.

questions.question: In the model configuration step, you select the llama-3-3-70b-instruct model for your Personal Health Advisor agent. Why do you select this model?
options: The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model supports multilingual summarization and rapid indexing.
The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model offers prebuilt responses for health-related scenarios.
The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model is optimized for structured data pipelines.
The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model is designed for instruction-following and interactive tasks.
questions.correct_answer: The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model is designed for instruction-following and interactive tasks.
questions.learning_goal: Understand why instruction-tuned models are suited for conversational agents.
common_wrong_answers: The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model offers prebuilt responses for health-related scenarios.
The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model supports multilingual summarization and rapid indexing.
The llama-3-3-70b-instruct model is optimized for structured data pipelines.

questions.question: You enable tools such as Google Search and WebCrawler in your agent setup. What key functionality do these tools provide for the Personal Health Advisor agent?
options: Allow access to APIs for fitness tracking devices.
Enable voice-based interaction with external assistants.
Allow real-time access to context, such as weather or user location.
Enable auto-correction for inaccurate user entries.
questions.correct_answer: Allow real-time access to context, such as weather or user location.
questions.learning_goal: Understand how external tools extend an agent’s real-time capabilities.
common_wrong_answers: Enable auto-correction for inaccurate user entries.
Allow access to APIs for fitness tracking devices.
Enable voice-based interaction with external assistants.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of writing a detailed prompt in the Instructions tab when setting up the Personal Health Advisor agent?
options: To configure fallback options in case of tool failure.
To train the model to improve performance on fitness datasets.
To store user preferences securely for future queries.
To clearly define the agent’s tasks and how it interacts with users.
questions.correct_answer: To clearly define the agent’s tasks and how it interacts with users.
questions.learning_goal: Understand how prompts define agent behavior and scope.
common_wrong_answers: To train the model to improve performance on fitness datasets.
To store user preferences securely for future queries.
To configure fallback options in case of tool failure.

questions.question: Why is a hosted runtime used in Google Colab during the AI agent setup?
options: To write and execute code using Google’s cloud infrastructure.
To store notebooks securely on GitHub.
To enable access to local system resources.
To block third-party libraries from being installed.
questions.correct_answer: To write and execute code using Google’s cloud infrastructure.
questions.learning_goal: Understand the role of hosted runtimes in cloud-based AI development.
common_wrong_answers: To enable access to local system resources.
To store notebooks securely on GitHub.
To block third-party libraries from being installed.

questions.question: After creating a Replicate account, you’re directed to the dashboard to generate an API token. Why is this Replicate API token necessary when working in a Colab notebook?
options: To store model outputs from Replicate in your local system.
To let the notebook call Replicate models securely while running.
To export AI agent data directly to your Replicate account.
To update Replicate models from within Google Colab.
questions.correct_answer: To let the notebook call Replicate models securely while running.
questions.learning_goal: Understand why API tokens are required for secure access to hosted models.
common_wrong_answers: To export AI agent data directly to your Replicate account.
To update Replicate models from within Google Colab.

questions.question: Why is tool calling used while evaluating an AI agent’s performance?
options: It enables the agent to edit its own prompt for better accuracy.
It allows the agent to display code outputs in the notebook.
It enables the agent to use pre-defined tools to answer specific questions.
It helps the agent ask follow-up questions to the user.
questions.correct_answer: It enables the agent to use pre-defined tools to answer specific questions.
questions.learning_goal: Understand how tool calling supports agent capability assessment.
common_wrong_answers: It helps the agent ask follow-up questions to the user.
It allows the agent to display code outputs in the notebook.

questions.question: Why do you need a Replicate account when setting up the environment for your agent?
options: To run AI models without the need for advanced hardware.
To save the Colab notebook in the cloud.
To track version history of your Jupyter notebooks.
To enable offline access to GitHub repositories.
questions.correct_answer: To run AI models without the need for advanced hardware.
questions.learning_goal: Understand the benefits of cloud-hosted AI model platforms.
common_wrong_answers: To track version history of your Jupyter notebooks.
To save the Colab notebook in the cloud.

questions.question: What aspects of the agent's responses are evaluated using MLflow?
options: Response time and storage usage
User engagement metrics and API latency
Accuracy, clarity, and relevance of final responses
Model training metrics and system resource usage
questions.correct_answer: Accuracy, clarity, and relevance of final responses
questions.learning_goal: Understand how MLflow is used to evaluate qualitative aspects of AI agent responses.
common_wrong_answers: Response time and storage usage
User engagement metrics and API latency
Model training metrics and system resource usage
